cards.front: How do you apply an ACL to restrict VTY (SSH/Telnet) access?
cards.back:
```
ip access-list standard MGMT-ALLOW
 permit 10.10.10.0 0.0.0.255
line vty 0 15
 access-class MGMT-ALLOW in
```
Note: VTY uses `access-class`, NOT `ip access-group`. Use a standard ACL — extended is unnecessary here.

cards.front: An ACL is applied 'in' on Gi0/0. Which traffic does it filter?
cards.back: Packets ARRIVING on Gi0/0 (entering the interface from the wire). 'out' filters packets LEAVING the interface. Same ACL applied 'in' vs 'out' has different effects depending on direction of expected flow.

cards.front: How do you see WHICH ACL is applied to which interface and direction?
cards.back: `show ip interface gi0/0` — look for 'Outgoing access list is X' and 'Inbound access list is Y' lines. Or `show running-config interface gi0/0` for the configuration view.

cards.front: What does `log` keyword do at the end of an ACL line?
cards.back: Generates a syslog/console message every time that line is matched. Useful for auditing/troubleshooting. Performance impact on busy networks. Example: `deny ip any any log`. Output: `%SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list 100 denied tcp 10.1.1.5 -> 192.168.1.10`.

cards.front: Can you have multiple ACLs on the same interface in the same direction?
cards.back: No — only ONE ACL per interface, per direction, per protocol. (You can have inbound + outbound on the same interface, just not two inbound.) If you need to combine logic, merge them into a single ACL.

cards.front: Common mistake: ACL blocking is not working. What 4 things do you check?
cards.back: (1) ACL applied to the right interface and direction (`show ip interface`). (2) Match criteria correct (wildcard mask?). (3) Order of entries — earlier permit might match first. (4) Hit counts on `show access-lists` — if zero, the traffic isn't reaching the ACL.

cards.front: What's the implicit handling of return traffic for an ACL — does Cisco track sessions?
cards.back: No — IOS standard ACLs are STATELESS. They don't remember outbound flows. To allow return traffic, you must explicitly permit it (e.g., `established` keyword for TCP, or open ephemeral port range). Stateful filtering needs Zone-Based Firewall or ASA.
